reading_md: Section 6.6.1 states that Table 149 defines the payload format for FLOGI and PLOGI ELSs and LS_ACCs, and that the parameter definitions apply to PLOGI, FLOGI, PLOGI LS_ACC, and FLOGI LS_ACC unless stated otherwise. Table 149 lays out the payload by word positions: word 0 is `ELS_Command code`; words 1-4 are `Common Service Parameters (16 bytes)`; words 5-6 are `Port_Name`; words 7-8 are `Node_ or Fabric_Name`; words 9-12 are `Class 1 and Class 6 Service Parameters (16 bytes)`; words 13-16 are `Class 2 Service Parameters (16 bytes)`; words 17-20 are `Class 3 Service Parameters (16 bytes)`; words 21-24 are `Obsolete`; words 25-28 are `Vendor Version Level (16 bytes)`; words 29-30 are `Services Availability (8 bytes)`; word 31 is `Login Extension Data Length`; words 32-61 are `Reserved`; words 62-63 are `Clock Synchronization QoS (8 bytes)`; and words 64 to n are `Login Extension Data (if any)`. The footnote on the table says `Services Availability`, `Login Extension Data Length`, `Clock Synchronization QoS`, and `Login Extension Data (if any)` are only present when the Payload Bit is set to one; if the Payload Bit is zero, those fields are absent and the payload is 116 bytes long. The preceding text gives a login procedure for large payloads: first send Login with Payload Bit zero; if the LS_ACC has Query Buffer Conditions bit one, issue RPBC; if both the RPBC LS_ACC ELS Receive Data Field Size and the Login LS_ACC Buffer-to-buffer Receive Data_Field Size are at least 256 bytes, issue Login with Payload Bit one. Section 6.6.1 also states that there are no separate Class 6 login Service Parameters because Class 1 service parameters are used instead.

claim_candidates.text: Table 149 defines the payload format for FLOGI and PLOGI ELSs and LS_ACCs, and its parameter definitions apply to PLOGI, FLOGI, PLOGI LS_ACC, and FLOGI LS_ACC unless stated otherwise.

evidence_refs.quote: Table 149 defines the Payload format for the FLOGI and PLOGI ELSs and the LS_ACCs.
tions of the parameters are applicable to PLOGI, FLOGI, PLOGI LS_ACC and FLOGI LS_ACC un-
less stated otherwise.

a These fields are only present when the Payload Bit (see 6.6.2.4.19) is set to one. When the Payload bit is
set to zero, these fields are not present in the Payload (i.e., the Payload is 116 bytes long).

claim_candidates.text: If a Login of 256 bytes or more is required and destination buffer conditions are unknown, the first step is to issue a Login request with the Payload Bit set to zero.

evidence_refs.quote: If a Login of 256 bytes or more is required when the buffer conditions of the destination port are un-
known, a timeout may be avoided with the following procedure:
1) issue a Login request with the Payload Bit (see 6.6.2.4.19) set to zero;

claim_candidates.text: In that large-login procedure, if the Query Buffer Conditions bit is set to one in the LS_ACC, the next step is to issue an RPBC ELS.

evidence_refs.quote: 2) if the Query Buffer Conditions bit (see 6.6.2.4.14) is set to one in the LS_ACC, issue an RPBC
ELS (see 4.2.37); and

claim_candidates.text: In that large-login procedure, if both the RPBC LS_ACC ELS Receive Data Field Size and the Login LS_ACC Buffer-to-buffer Receive Data_Field Size are at least 256 bytes, a Login request is issued with the Payload Bit set to one.

evidence_refs.quote: 3) if both the ELS Receive Data Field Size field in the RPBC LS_ACC Payload and the Buffer-to-
buffer Receive Data_Field Size field in the Login LS_ACC Payload are at least 256 bytes, issue
a Login request with the Payload Bit set to one.
